Conference talk ideas for NeurIPS 2026

1. Batch correction as a distributional alignment problem.
   Frame scVI-style approaches as optimal transport in latent space.
2. Why scRNA-seq benchmarks are broken.
   Most papers test on the same three datasets; overfitting the benchmarks
   is now the dominant failure mode.
3. The cost of zero-shot: a case study in microscopy foundation models.
   When does fine-tuning pay off vs. when is zero-shot good enough?
